programme.title: Germán por un día
programme.desc: Germán gets sick and the neighbors decide to help him with the building work. Magdalena invents a trip to Paris to show off to her friend.

programme.title: Embarazo
programme.desc: Germán has the task of planting small trees that the mayor's office has given them. Someone in the building is pregnant.

programme.title: ¡Voy a ser papá!
programme.desc: Silvia le confiesa a Luis que está embarazada y él comienza a comprar muchas cosas para el bebé. Magdalena y su familia se vuelven ricos y comienzan a derrochar su dinero.

programme.title: La casa de las Lomas
programme.desc: Magdalena les dice a los vecinos que ella y su familia se mudarán a Las Lomas y le pide a Luis comprarle una mansión.

programme.title: Perdimos todo
programme.desc: Luis comienza a molestar a Silvia todo el tiempo queriendo controlar lo que hace para cuidar del bebé. Magdalena invierte todo su dinero en una empresa fantasma y pierde todo.

programme.title: Luisito, corazón de plomo
programme.desc: Magda le dice a Luis que no pueden mudarse a su mansión y le pide de vuelta las llaves del departamento, pero él se niega a dejarlos vivir en en edificio otra vez y todos los vecinos tratan de convencerlo de darles otra oportunidad.

programme.title: El muñeco vudú
programme.desc: Germán y Jorjáis encuentran un muñeco vudú, asumen que alguien está haciendo brujería en el edificio. Pedro, Cris y Vanesa tienen problemas con el WiFi de su departamento.

programme.title: Síntomas empáticos
programme.desc: Luis descubre que es él (y no Silvia), quien tiene los síntomas del embarazo. Los vecinos encuentran en la calle carteles pegados de: "Se busca a Jorjáis".

programme.title: La hija de Jorjais
programme.desc: Una mujer le pregunta a Germán sobre el paradero de Jorjáis, y él se sorprende al descubrir que ella es su hija. Vanessa cree que tiene la maldición de la soltería.

programme.title: Niño o niña
programme.desc: Luis ya no sabe que hacer con Silvia porque tiene muchos cambios de humor por el embarazo, pero sus vecinos lo convencen de darle una sorpresa que jamás olvidará.

programme.title: Conserje aprendiz
programme.desc: Lorena acepta hacer un casting para interpretar el papel de La Doña. Germán decide enseñarle a Borrego a ser un gran conserje.

programme.title: El perfume
programme.desc: Magdalena se aprovecha de las náuseas que siente Silvia por el embarazo para que Luis le compre todo lo que ella quiere.

programme.title: Apolonio y la víbora
programme.desc: Vanesa lleva una serpiente al edificio y se le escapa. Ahora tiene que recuperarla sin que los vecinos se enteren. Silvia y Luis toman un curso psicoprofiláctico con una chica que resulta ser la ex de Luis.

programme.title: La chapa
programme.desc: Luis descompone la chapa de la reja, quedando él y Silvia afuera y el resto de los vecinos adentro. En lo que reparan la chapa, los vecinos usan a Luis de mandadero.

programme.title: Los tocatimbres
programme.desc: Germán cree que los timbres del edificio están embrujados, pues alguien los hace sonar constantemente. Luis intenta colocar unas repisas en el cuarto del bebé.

programme.title: Arturo más pordiosero
programme.desc: Los López Pérez se hacen pasar por pordioseros para recibir caridad. Pedro lleva varios días sin bañarse y Cris le exige que lo haga.

programme.title: El parto
programme.desc: Silvia comienza el trabajo de parto en el departamento, no hay tiempo para llegar al hospital. Luis recibirá al bebé con la ayuda de los vecinos.

programme.title: Vecinos consejeros
programme.desc: El hijo de Silvia y Luis no para de llorar y ya no saben qué hacer. Los vecinos les aconsejan cómo cuidar al bebé mientras Germán improvisa un gimnasio en el vestíbulo.

programme.title: Sortilegio
programme.desc: Alejandro returns home after a terrible accident to discover that he is married to Maria Jose, a poor, innocent girl who was deceived by Alejandro's brother Bruno. Bruno used Alejandro's name to marry Maria Jose, soon after he sent to kill his brother in hopes that Maria Jose would inherit Alejandro's fortune. But when Maria Jose arrives at the mansion she realizes that Bruno lied to her and used her. She is also scared when Alejandro is found alive and returns home. Doctors tell Alejandro that he suffers from memory loss but he is convinced he never met Maria Jose before and goes in search of the truth, but during this time and Maria Jose actually fall in love, while Bruno and Maura (Alejandro's ex-girlfriend) plot to break them up. Meanwhile, the brothers' mother Victoria has fallen in love with Fernando--Alejandro's best friend, so closer to his age than hers.

Bruno used Alejandro's name to marry Maria Jose, soon after he sent to kill his brother in hopes that Maria Jose would inherit Alejandro's fortune. But when Maria Jose arrives at the mansion she realizes that Bruno lied to her and used her. She is also scared when Alejandro is found alive and returns home. Doctors tell Alejandro that he suffers from memory loss but he is convinced he never met Maria Jose before and goes in search of the truth, but during this time and Maria Jose actually fall in love, while Bruno and Maura (Alejandro's ex-girlfriend) plot to break them up. Meanwhile, the brothers' mother Victoria has fallen in love with Fernando--Alejandro's best friend, so closer to his age than hers.

programme.title: MeatEater - Louisiana Ducks
programme.desc: Steven Rinella joins Jean-Paul Bourgeois and Ronnie Collins in Louisiana‚ Mississippi Delta for a duck hunt. Surrounded by the unique landscape of the Delta, they target multiple duck species. After a successful hunt, the team returns to camp, where Jean-Paul prepares a traditional Cajun meal featuring the day‚ catch. Experience the cultural tradition of turning fresh game into flavorful, local dishes like duck gumbo and crawfish boil.

programme.title: MeatEater - New Mexico Turkey
programme.desc: Steven Rinella heads to New Mexico's Gila Wilderness with his friend Carl Malcolm to hunt Merriam's turkeys. Set against a backdrop of stunning landscapes and rich history, they explore the first federally protected wilderness in the world. Although few and far between, the duo find gobbling birds through hard scouting and perserverence.

programme.title: Assembly Required with Stacey Abrams (Obama's Library, Trump's UFC 250, and Two Very Different Americas)
programme.desc: This week, guest host Melissa Murray, host of Crooked Media’s Strict Scrutiny podcast is joined by Erin Ryan, co-host of Crooked Media’s Hysteria. They juxtapose the Obama Presidential Center opening with Trump’s UFC event celebrating his 80th birthday, and talk about the two very different visions of our country that those events (and their attendants) represent. They also touch on Trump’s dismal approval ratings, the biggest takeaways from primary battles in states like New York and Georgia, the positive vibes at the World Cup, and why the right just can’t move on from attacking pride month.

programme.title: Runaway Country with Alex Wagner (Grifter In Chief)
programme.desc: The levels of corruption Americans are witnessing are both historic and astounding. This week, Alex speaks to Pro Publica reporter Robert Faturechi, who broke the story that the Department of Defense gave a $620,000,000 loan to a company affiliated with Donald Trump Jr. Then she’s joined by Pod Save the World host Ben Rhodes to talk about how everything from the UFC cage match on the White House lawn, to Ivanka Trump’s island development in Albania are indicative of the unprecedented grift, and limitless appetite for self enrichment of the Trump family. Be sure to pick up a copy of Ben’s new book, All We Say: The Battle for American Identity: A History of the United States in 15 Speeches.

programme.title: Runaway Country with Alex Wagner (Shady JD (Vance))
programme.desc: Is there anything that Vice President JD Vance hasn’t messed up? From political messaging, to lecturing the Pope about theology, Vance’s public appearances are often cringe inducing. But this week, Alex digs into the pernicious actions he’s taking behind the scenes. First she’s joined by California Attorney General Rob Bonta who reveals how much damage Vance’s anti-fraud task force is doing to legitimate businesses, and impacting those who need their services like hospice care. Then Alex speaks to Majority Report host Sam Seder about what it means to have a dishonest and bad actor like Vance waiting in the wings to be the next President.

programme.title: Pod Save America (Gavin Newsom Is Finally Comfortable with Himself)
programme.desc: California Governor Gavin Newsom sits down with Jon and Tommy before a live audience in Los Angeles to discuss Trump's war on Iran, the crowded California gubernatorial primary, and his new book Young Man in a Hurry: A Memoir of Discovery. The governor talks about his close childhood friendship with the Getty family, issuing the first same-sex marriage licenses nationwide as San Francisco Mayor, and his surprising childhood pet, Potter the otter.

programme.title: Runaway Country with Alex Wagner (The Other MAGA War….At Home)
programme.desc: While the Trump administration wages war against Iran and broadens their military scope in the northern hemisphere, there are more subtle campaigns being pushed here at home – like the one to convince women to do nothing more than have babies and stay at home. This week, Alex speaks to former trad wife and host of “The Wake Her Up Podcast”, Sharon Johnson, about her upbringing in the Mormon Church, what it was like to discover she could have agency and choice in her late thirties, and how she feels about trad wives trending. Then Alex is joined by host of Crooked’s “Hysteria” podcast, Erin Ryan, to talk about the broader political goals behind the Heritage Foundation’s Project 2026 plan, and how it attempts to punish women who don’t fit their ideals.

programme.title: Strict Scrutiny (SCOTUS Not Cool With Colorado Ban on Conversion Therapy)
programme.desc: Today the Supreme Court issued its opinion in Chiles v. Salazer, the case involving a Colorado ban on conversion therapy. Leah is joined by Shannon Minter, Legal Director at the National Center for LGBTQ Rights, to break it down.

programme.title: Pod Save the World (Starmer Brexits)
programme.desc:
This week Tommy and Ben catch up on the latest in Iran negotiations, react to Keir Starmer’s resignation, and continue to celebrate World Cup fandom.
First they walk through the text of the US-Iran memorandum of understanding — because now it’s clear why Trump didn't want anyone to see it. The guys break down everything Iran got, including sanctions relief and billions in unfrozen assets, compared to the very vague assurances the US received that Iran won't build a nuclear weapon and will keep the Strait of Hormuz open. Then they go over the latest negotiations in Switzerland, disagreements over what was agreed to, and why the war in Lebanon remains the biggest threat to its success. Across the pond, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced his resignation after Andy Burnham's decisive by-election win, setting up the UK to have its seventh prime minister in ten years. The guys also dive into Israel's increasingly close and diplomatically fraught relationship with Somaliland, and Colombia's razor-thin runoff election, which was won by a right-wing candidate who calls himself “El Tigre.” And finally, the World Cup remains an absolute joy — Ben and Tommy go over some of their favorite moments like underdog Cape Verde holding Spain and Uruguay to draws, Scottish fans taking over Boston and Miami, and Uzbek fans riding into a Houston stadium on horseback. At the end of the show, Ben speaks to Washington Post columnist Rana Ayyub about the Cockroach Movement in India, and how the war with Iran has spilled over into the country’s politics.

programme.title: The Price Of Apples
programme.desc: Oliver (Eddie Albert)'s apple crop is ready to be harvested, and he decides to take them to market himself and make a killing. Of course, getting to market before the price drops proves a little more difficult than Oliver anticipates.

programme.title: What's In A Name?
programme.desc: Ralph (Mary Grace Canfield) becomes enamored of Mr. Kimball (Alvy Moore), the county agent. However, though she feminizes herself and gets all gussied up, he refuses to go out with a girl named Ralph. Can Oliver solve the situation legally?

programme.title: The Day Of Decision
programme.desc: Six months is up! Now, Lisa (Eva Gabor) gets to decide whether she and Oliver (Eddie Albert) will remain on the farm or go back to New York and their Park Avenue lifestyle! Will this be the final straw for Green Acres?

programme.title: The Deputy
programme.desc: Sam Drucker (Frank Cady) is leaving on a much-needed two-week vacation and talks Oliver (Eddie Albert) into assuming his duties as deputy sheriff. But Oliver soon learns enforcing the law isn't quite as easy as it looks.

programme.title: The Ballad Of Molly Turgiss
programme.desc: Whatever you do, don't say her name and especially while playing checkers down at Drucker's! An old hag named Molly Turgiss (Renie Riano), who died twenty years before the Douglases (Eddie Albert, Eva Gabor) came to town, might be haunting them.

programme.title: Never Look A Gift Tractor In The Mouth
programme.desc: It's a few days before Oliver (Eddie Albert)'s birthday, and Lisa (Eva Gabor) doesn't know quite what to get him�until the old Hoyt Clagwell goes kerflooey! So, she goes out and buys the biggest and fanciest tractor she can find.

programme.title: Send A Boy To College
programme.desc: Oliver (Eddie Albert) discovers that Eb (Tom Lester) has a way with sick animals after Eb cures Arnold, the pig, and Alice, the chicken, and offers to put him through veterinary college. But will life in Green Acres ever be the same without Ed?!

programme.title: Horse? What Horse?
programme.desc: A horse follows Oliver (Eddie Albert) home, but every time he tries to show it to his wife, the horse disappears. Lisa (Eva Gabor) assumes that her husband is imagining things�and as more animals show up, Oliver begins to think so too.

programme.title: The Rains Came
programme.desc: Mr. Haney (Pat Buttram) takes Oliver (Eddie Albert) to Pixley Superior Municipal Court and sues him for non-payment of a vital contract over the little matter of some rain�or lack thereof. Does Mr. Haney know who he's up against? Does Oliver?

programme.title: Culture
programme.desc: The Third-Tuesday-of-Every-Month-Culture-Committee of the Every-Other-Wednesday-Afternoon-Discussion-Club decides that Hooterville needs some "culture" in the form of a symphony orchestra. And Lisa (Eva Gabor) may just know the perfect conductor.

programme.title: Uncle Ollie
programme.desc: Oliver's (Eddie Albert) nephew Charles (Don Edmonds) arrives for a visit, and Oliver is completely shaken up when the boy turns out to be a long-haired, motorcycle-ridin', hippy-dippy type. But, boy, does he know a thing or two about engines.

programme.title: Wings Over Hooterville
programme.desc: Oliver dreams of entering his crop in the Corn Festival, but the dreaded "Bing Bug" invades Hooterville's farms. After learning of his service as a WWII pilot, the locals volunteer him for the perilous task of flying the town's only crop duster.

programme.title: How To See South America By Bus
programme.desc: When a lovely, young and successful farmer comes to Oliver (Eddie Albert) for his legal counsel, Lisa (Eva Gabor) quickly becomes jealous of the two spending time together and becomes determined to break up what she sees as a budding romance.

programme.title: The Ugly Duckling
programme.desc: When the Monroe Brothers show up to fix the Douglas's closet, Lisa learns that Ralph is devastated because her true love has stood her up on another date. Lisa's solution: an intensive, two-day beauty treatment for Ralph�and the barn for Oliver!

programme.title: One Of Our Assemblymen Is Missing
programme.desc: Oliver (Eddie Albert) takes to his soap box, delivering fiery, patriotic speeches after getting a bill from the State Farm Unattached Duty Tax for an outrageous $12.03! But no one can figure out why the tax is levied in the first place.

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Lisa (Eva Gabor) is curious to learn about the book that Oliver (Eddie Albert) is reading. It is the "real-life" story of pioneers Gus and Etta Thompson. The episode flashes back to the 19th century with Oliver appearing as Gus and Lisa as Etta.

programme.title: Eb Discovers The Birds And The Bees
programme.desc: Eb (Tom Lester) seeks dating advice from the Douglas's after laying eyes on former classmate Betty Jo Bradley from "petticoat Junction." Oliver's well-intentioned guidance has mixed results when Eb hilariously misconstrues the advice he's given.

programme.title: The Addams Family Splurges
programme.desc: The family would like to vacation on the moon, but the billion dollar travel cost is slightly prohibitive. Uncle Fester volunteers $21.13 and Morticia, using Gomez's new supercomputer, suggests placing a $2,000 bet on the daily double horse race.

programme.title: Cousin Itt Visits The Addams Family
programme.desc: Cousin Itt is advocated as curator of the local zoo. Gomez uses his influence with Park Commissioner Fisk (guest star Alan Reed) to promote Itt for the job, but the zookeepers are convinced the Addamses' cousin should be confined to a cage.

programme.title: The Addams Family In Court
programme.desc: Grandmama (Blossom Rock) is hauled off to jail for setting up a fortune-telling booth in the Addamses' living room. Gomez (John Astin) volunteers to act as attorney, and the court session becomes a shambles.

programme.title: Amnesia In The Addams Family
programme.desc: Gomez (John Astin) loses his memory after hitting his head; he fails to recognize Morticia (Carolyn Jones), and says the house looks like a condemned museum. The Addamses take turns hitting Gomez on the head trying to help him regain his memory.

programme.title: Thing Is Missing
programme.desc: Thing disappears from his box. Failing to find a single trace of Thing, Gomez (John Astin) conducts an investigation--with all members of the household under suspicion. Also starring Carolyn Jones, Jackie Coogan, and Ted Cassidy.

programme.title: Crisis In The Addams Family
programme.desc: Uncle Fester applies for a job as an insurance salesman with the same company which is threatening to cancel the Addamses' policy (because Fester keeps blowing things up). Fester convinces the company interviewer that he is qualified for the job.

programme.title: Lurch And His Harpsicord
programme.desc: Lurch becomes inconsolable when Gomez donates the family antique harpsichord to a collector. Gomez and Morticia offer Lurch a balalaika and a set of drums. When this proves unsatisfactory, Gomez and Fester decide to build a new harpsichord.

programme.title: Morticia, The Breadwinner
programme.desc: When Morticia hears the stock market has crashed, she assumes that Gomez has been financially wiped out and rallies the family to help make money. Morticia gives lessons, Lurch and Fester run an escort service, and the kids run a poison drink stand.

programme.title: The Addams Family And The Spaceman
programme.desc: While enjoying a moonlight picnic and snail hunt, the Addams Family is mistaken for Martians by uniformed investigators from Mysterious Space Objects Headquarters. In turn, Gomez (John Astin) and the clan regard their inquisitors with suspicion.

programme.title: My Son, The Chimp
programme.desc: An organ grinder's fugitive monkey leads Fester to believe that he has turned Pugsley into a monkey. Morticia and Gomez treat the monkey as though he were their son, welcoming him to the dinner table and giving him Pugsley's chores.

programme.title: Morticia's Favorite Charity
programme.desc: Moriticia's favorite charity is having an auction--so Morticia persuades her family to part with things to sell. Donations include Wednesday's headless doll, the old flogging table, a stuffed vulture and Uncle Fester's dynamite.

programme.title: Progress And The Addams Family
programme.desc: The Addams Family ignores notices that their house is to be condemned to make way for a freeway. When sudden explosions cannot be traced to any member of the family but, instead, to city workmen, Gomez is forced to consider moving the house.

programme.title: Uncle Fester's Toupee

programme.title: Cousin Itt And The Vocational Counselor

programme.title: Lurch, The Teenage Idol

programme.title: The Winning Of Morticia Addams

programme.title: My Fair Cousin Itt

programme.title: The Crisis of Truth in American Politics — with Sam Harris

programme.title: Euphoria Has Taken Over The Markets
programme.desc: Scott Galloway and Ed Elson break down the biggest stories in business and markets.

programme.title: Why Is Everyone So Emotionally Fragile?
programme.desc: Emotional maturity is a difficult thing to truly come by. Making your needs known, setting boundaries, being able to disappoint people without being afraid. If it's such an important skill, why is it so hard to discover how to develop it? Expect to learn how Whitney has been changed since becoming a mother, why Whitney has been thinking about circumcision so much, what codependence is and how to overcome it, why your niceness might be narcissism in disguise, why the news and memes are moving at such an insane velocity right now and much more...

programme.title: The Biology Of Focus, Success & Long-Term Energy
programme.desc: From personal dramas to scientific uproars, it's been a wild year for the biggest health & fitness podcaster in the world. And today we get to discover his biggest new insights about life, relationships and protocols. Expect to learn whether you actually should drink coffee within 90 minutes of waking up, how to get the best sleep of your life according to the latest science, how to become a morning person, what Andrew has learned about the perils of fame and public scrutiny, what new research says on the world of longevity supplements, why you should always do your research when testing with peptides and much more…

programme.title: The Lost Art of Reinventing Yourself
programme.desc: Expect to learn what “Don’t half-ass it” means, the story of how Matthew got his iconic starting role in Dazed & Confused, how to see the upside during any crisis, why having a sense of humour should be your default emotion, McConaughey’s own version of his Lonely Chapter, when you should listen to your gut versus your head, why McConaughey turned down $14.5M to pursue something great, Matthew's reflections on the 10 year anniversary of Interstellar, lesson on finding the perfect partner, and much more…

programme.title: You Were Never Taught How to Be a Man
programme.desc: Hustle culture often tells us that working harder is the only way to succeed—grinding through 80-hour weeks on 4 hours of sleep per night. But what if the real solution isn’t about working harder, but finding balance? What if true success lies in slowing down, savouring life, smoking a cigar on the porch occasionally and being fully present. How differently might we define success then? Expect to learn who Dry Creek Dewayne’s is, his backstory and upbringing, how to un-harden yourself, how any person can overcome & control their anger, what Dewayne has learned from working with horses, the dangers of being out of balance in work & life, how to have a better relationship with the voice in your head, how to find the right partner & why men need to learn to treat women better, why bad fathers are partly to blame for the downfall of America’s culture, what a day in the life of Dewayne’s life is like and much more…

programme.desc: A UFO looms over a couple's home, and a ghostly playmate torments a family.

programme.title: Five Miles From Hell
programme.desc: The TWC investigate the dark and angry spirits haunting Port Townsend, WA.

programme.title: Darkness in the Underworld
programme.desc: The TWC investigate the mysterious underworld of Pendleton, OR.

programme.title: The Grell
programme.desc: The Grell had come to earth not as equals, but as slaves. Now, an uprising has gripped the world in fear, and a high-ranking government official (Ted Shackelford), who is lost in Grell territory, experiences firsthand the ugly face of racism.

programme.title: The Other Side
programme.desc: Dr. Eberhardt (Ralph Macchio) discovers a way for comatose patients to communicate with each other by interfacing with a computer. But when he hooks himself up to the machine, he discovers a cyberspace "paradise" he may never be able to leave!

programme.title: Joyride
programme.desc: Cliff Robertson -- who starred in the very first episode of the classic series -- returns to the Outer Limits as an aging astronaut whose 1963 mission was a career-ending disaster. If he can just get back to space, perhaps he can clear his name.

programme.title: The Human Operators
programme.desc: Like other Star Fleet vessels, Ship (voice of Malcolm McDowell) has evolved and now controls the only human (Jack Noseworthy) aboard. But the relationship takes a dramatic turn when Man meets Woman and fights machine in a desperate bid for freedom!

programme.title: Blank Slate
programme.desc: Do our memories define our identity? If we are merely the sum of our experiences, what do we become if those experiences are lost? Hope Wilson (Robbie Chong) faces this question when she meets a man who has purposely erased his own memories.

programme.title: What Will The Neighbors Think?
programme.desc: Courtesy of a freak accident (and The Outer Limits), an agoraphobe (Jane Adams) begins to hear the private thoughts of all of the tenants in her building...the good, the bad and the steamy. And it seems at least one of them may want to kill her.

programme.title: The Shroud
programme.desc: A scientist -- who is also a religious zealot (David Ogden Stiers) -- secretly implants an embryo created with DNA from the Shroud of Turin into a young woman (Samantha Mathis) in order to scientifically engineer the Second Coming of Christ.

programme.title: Ripper
programme.desc: The Outer Limits come to Victorian Whitechapel when a London doctor (Cary Elwes) races to capture the alien creature responsible for the Jack the Ripper murders. But when the alien frames the good doctor for the murders, his time may be up.

programme.title: Tribunal
programme.desc: The son (Saul Rubinek) of a concentration camp survivor (Peter Boretski) gets some unique help in proving the guilt of a former Nazi (Jan Rubes) living in the U.S....courtesy of a time traveler (Alex Daikun) and, of course, The Outer Limits.

programme.title: Summit
programme.desc: The planets Earth and Dregocia struggle to re-establish a fragile trust after a summit goes tragically wrong. Can a diplomat (Marcia Cross) calm the hawkish nature of both species…and can she overcome her own prejudices to save both worlds?
